NoteText: 45-year-old Hispanic female (G2P2) presents with 4-month history of irregular vaginal bleeding, post-coital spotting, and malodorous vaginal discharge. Reports mild left-sided pelvic pain for the past 6 weeks. No weight loss. No urinary or bowel symptoms. PMH: Obesity (BMI 31.2), mild intermittent asthma (albuterol PRN). No prior surgeries. Social history: born in Mexico, lives in Chicago, works as a school teacher, non-smoker, occasional alcohol. OB/GYN history: menarche age 12, regular menses, last Pap smear 8 years ago (reportedly normal), no prior abnormal Paps, no history of STIs. Sexual history: 2 lifetime partners. Family history: mother with Type 2 DM, father with HTN, no family history of cancer. ECOG performance status 0. Physical exam: Speculum exam reveals a 4 cm exophytic, friable cervical mass involving the anterior and posterior lip of the cervix with contact bleeding. Bimanual exam: mass palpable extending to the right parametrium, not reaching the pelvic sidewall. No left parametrial involvement. Rectovaginal exam confirms right parametrial extension. No cul-de-sac nodularity. No inguinal lymphadenopathy. Punch biopsy of cervical mass performed in office and sent to pathology. HPV co-testing sent. Labs ordered: CBC, CMP, SCC-Ag, HIV.

GYN Oncology consultation. Cervical biopsy results reviewed: invasive squamous cell carcinoma, keratinizing type. HPV testing: HPV-16 positive. SCC-Ag elevated at 8.2 ng/mL (normal <1.5). HIV negative. Reviewed with patient in detail. MRI pelvis ordered for local staging. PET/CT ordered for systemic staging. Plan: 1) Complete staging workup. 2) EUA with cystoscopy if needed based on imaging. 3) Present at GYN tumor board for treatment planning. 4) Likely concurrent chemoradiation + brachytherapy for Stage IIB disease based on clinical exam. Social work referral placed for psychosocial support.

Imaging results reviewed. MRI pelvis confirms 4.2 x 3.8 x 3.5 cm cervical mass with parametrial invasion on the right side (not reaching pelvic sidewall). Tumor extends into the upper one-third of vagina anteriorly. No bladder or rectal wall invasion. Right internal iliac lymph node 1.6 cm and right obturator lymph node 1.3 cm, both FDG-avid on PET/CT (SUVmax 8.4 and 6.2 respectively). Left external iliac node 0.9 cm, FDG-avid (SUVmax 4.8). No para-aortic lymphadenopathy. No distant metastases. Staging: FIGO 2018 Stage IIB (parametrial invasion + pelvic LN involvement). Discussed at multidisciplinary conference. Recommendation: definitive concurrent cisplatin-based chemoradiation with brachytherapy. Not a surgical candidate given parametrial involvement.

Staging confirmed as FIGO IIB cervical squamous cell carcinoma, HPV-16 positive. Discussed treatment plan with patient at length including risks, benefits, alternatives, and expected outcomes. Treatment plan: 1) External beam radiation therapy (EBRT) 45 Gy in 25 fractions to the pelvis with concurrent weekly cisplatin 40 mg/m2. 2) Followed by MRI-guided brachytherapy (intracavitary + interstitial) to deliver cumulative EQD2 >85 Gy to HRCTV. 3) Total treatment duration target: 56 days or less. Pre-treatment: Dental clearance obtained. Audiogram baseline normal. Creatinine clearance 98 mL/min. Port placement scheduled. Fertility counseling offered - patient declined (family complete). Radiation simulation scheduled for 2025-12-01.

Radiation oncology initial consultation and CT simulation. Patient positioned supine on wingboard with full bladder protocol. IV and oral contrast administered. CT simulation acquired with 3mm slices from T10 through proximal femurs. MRI fusion performed for target volume delineation. GTV: 4.2 cm cervical mass with right parametrial extension. CTV includes cervix, uterus, parametria bilaterally, upper vagina, and involved pelvic lymph node stations (bilateral common iliac, external iliac, internal iliac, obturator, presacral). PTV = CTV + 7mm margin. OAR constraints: rectum V45 <50%, bladder V45 <50%, bowel bag V45 <195cc, femoral heads V40 <10%. Plan: IMRT 45 Gy in 25 fractions with SIB to involved nodes (55 Gy/25fx). Weekly concurrent cisplatin 40 mg/m2 x 5 weeks. Brachytherapy to follow EBRT.

Cycle 1 Day 1 concurrent cisplatin: Cisplatin 40 mg/m2 IV (68 mg based on BSA 1.70 m2, weight 78 kg, height 162 cm). Pre-hydration: 1L NS with 20 mEq KCl and 2g MgSO4 over 2 hours. Pre-medications: ondansetron 16 mg IV, dexamethasone 12 mg IV, fosaprepitant 150 mg IV. Post-hydration: 1L NS over 2 hours. Creatinine 0.72 mg/dL, GFR >90. ANC 4.2, Hgb 12.1, Plt 278. No infusion reactions. Patient tolerated well. Radiation therapy (fraction 1 of 25) also delivered today. RTC weekly for cisplatin x 5 total cycles concurrent with EBRT.

Cycle 2 Day 1 concurrent cisplatin. Labs: WBC 5.8, ANC 3.6, Hgb 11.4, Plt 252, Cr 0.78. Cisplatin 40 mg/m2 administered per protocol. Radiation fraction 6 of 25 delivered. Patient reports Grade 1 nausea (controlled with ondansetron), Grade 1 diarrhea (2-3 loose stools/day, managed with loperamide), Grade 1 fatigue. No mucositis. Adequate PO intake. ECOG 0. Continue treatment.

Cycle 3 Day 1 concurrent cisplatin. Labs: WBC 4.9, ANC 2.8, Hgb 10.8, Plt 218, Cr 0.81. Cisplatin 40 mg/m2 administered. Radiation fraction 11 of 25. Patient reports Grade 2 diarrhea (4-6 stools/day, now on scheduled loperamide), Grade 1 radiation dermatitis (erythema in inguinal folds), Grade 1 dysuria. Started topical silver sulfadiazine for dermatitis. Adequate hydration counseled. Monitoring renal function closely.

Cycle 4 Day 1 concurrent cisplatin. Labs: WBC 3.8, ANC 2.1, Hgb 10.2, Plt 189, Cr 0.85. Cisplatin 40 mg/m2 administered. Radiation fraction 16 of 25. Grade 2 diarrhea persisting (managed), Grade 2 radiation dermatitis (moist desquamation in right inguinal fold - wound care initiated), Grade 1 fatigue, Grade 1 nausea. SCC-Ag trending down to 3.8 ng/mL from 8.2 at baseline. Repeat MRI scheduled after completion of EBRT to plan brachytherapy.

Cycle 5 Day 1 (final cycle) concurrent cisplatin. Labs: WBC 3.2, ANC 1.8, Hgb 9.6, Plt 165, Cr 0.88. ANC above threshold, proceeding with cisplatin 40 mg/m2. Radiation fraction 21 of 25. Patient with Grade 2 fatigue, Grade 2 diarrhea (improving), Grade 2 radiation dermatitis (stable with wound care), Grade 1 cystitis. Oral intake adequate with supplemental nutrition shakes. Weight stable at 76 kg (-2 kg from baseline). Plans: Complete EBRT fractions 22-25 this week. MRI for brachytherapy planning next week. Brachytherapy scheduled to begin 2026-01-14.

Completed 25/25 fractions EBRT (45 Gy whole pelvis, 55 Gy SIB to involved nodes). All 5 weekly cisplatin cycles delivered without dose reduction or treatment breaks. MRI pelvis for brachytherapy planning obtained today: cervical mass decreased to 2.1 x 1.8 x 1.6 cm (from 4.2 cm, >50% reduction). Right parametrial thickening improved. Pelvic lymph nodes decreased in size. Excellent partial response to chemoradiation. Plan: MRI-guided intracavitary/interstitial brachytherapy x 5 fractions (HDR Ir-192), 5.5 Gy per fraction x 5 = 27.5 Gy. HRCTV target: cumulative EQD2 >=87 Gy. First application 2026-01-14.

Brachytherapy application #1 (of 5). Patient taken to brachytherapy suite under conscious sedation. Foley catheter placed. Uterine tandem and ring applicator inserted under ultrasound guidance. Interstitial needles placed in residual right parametrial disease (3 needles). CT/MRI fusion obtained for treatment planning. HRCTV delineated. D90 HRCTV = 5.8 Gy this fraction. OAR doses within constraints: D2cc rectum 3.2 Gy, D2cc bladder 4.1 Gy, D2cc sigmoid 2.8 Gy. HDR Ir-192 treatment delivered. No complications. Patient discharged home same day with analgesics and stool softeners.

Completed all 5 brachytherapy fractions (1/14, 1/16, 1/21, 1/23, 1/28). Cumulative doses: HRCTV D90 EQD2 = 88.4 Gy (target met). Cumulative D2cc EQD2: rectum 68.2 Gy (limit 75), bladder 76.8 Gy (limit 90), sigmoid 62.4 Gy (limit 75). All OAR constraints met. Total treatment duration: 52 days (within 56-day target). Patient tolerated brachytherapy well. Current symptoms: Grade 1 fatigue, Grade 1 diarrhea (resolving), Grade 1 vaginal discharge. Radiation dermatitis resolving. Plan: 1) Post-treatment PET/CT at 3 months (April 2026). 2) SCC-Ag every 3 months. 3) Pelvic exam + Pap every 3 months x 2 years. 4) HPV co-testing annually. 5) Discussed late effects: vaginal stenosis (prescribed vaginal dilator), bowel/bladder changes, lymphedema risk, premature menopause. 6) Referral to survivorship clinic.
